pi_items.verified_value: Ring of Fire dominance: 90% of world earthquakes occur in Ring of Fire; 2024 M6.0+ earthquakes concentrated in Pacific region (Japan, Taiwan, Indonesia, Philippines, Mexico) with highest concentration in subduction zones
pi_items.source: USGS Ring of Fire documentation and 2024 earthquake distribution data
USGS data: 2024 had only 99 earthquakes exceeding magnitude 6, lowest since 1982
USGS Earthquake Hazards Program official website

privileged_info_summary: The task requires searching USGS earthquake data for 2024 with M6.0 or greater. The actual 2024 USGS catalog contains exactly 99 such earthquakes (the lowest since 1982), significantly fewer than the verifier's expectations of 120-130. The strongest earthquake was M7.5 in Noto Peninsula, Japan (January 1, 2024), followed by M7.4 near Hualien, Taiwan (April 3, 2024). Geographic patterns show 90% concentration in the Pacific Ring of Fire, with major events in Japan, Taiwan, Indonesia, Philippines, and Mexico. The USGS CSV format includes more headers than expected (22 fields including time, latitude, longitude, mag, place plus magType, depth, net, id, etc.). The task requires downloading this real, publicly available data from earthquake.usgs.gov and analyzing it.
